name: Pie Shell for Pecan Pie
recipeIngredient: plus extra for dough and surface
8 tablespoons chilled unsalted butter, cut into 1/4-inch pats
2 tablespoons vegetable shortening, frozen solid and cut
1 large egg white, mixed with ice water
1 large egg yolk, beaten with 1/8 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Scatter butter and shortening over dry ingredients and pulse until mixture resembles coarse cornmeal, about 10 to 15 seconds. Turn the mixture into a medium bowl.
2. Sprinkle egg white mixture over flour mixture and, using a folding motion with a rubber spatula, mix until dough forms a ball. Shape dough into a 4-inch disk by hand, then flatten into a disk in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour or up to 2 days.
3. Roll dough on a lightly floured surface into a 13-inch circle and transfer to a 9-inch pie pan, preferably glass. Press dough into corners and sides of the pan, being careful not to stretch it. Trim edges of dough to create a 1/2-inch overhang. Tuck overhanging dough under so that the folded edge is flush with the rim of the pan. Flute the edge.
4. Chill shell until firm, about 1 hour. Prick sides and bottom with a fork and line the entire shell with heavy-duty aluminum foil, pressing foil firmly against the shell and extending it over the fluted rim. Prick foil with a fork and return the shell to the refrigerator while heating the oven.
5. Adjust oven rack to center position and heat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Bake, pressing gently if necessary to flatten any puffing, until crust is firmly set, about 15 minutes. Remove foil and continue baking until bottom begins to color, about 10 minutes longer. Remove from oven, brush sides and bottom with egg yolk, and return to the oven until yolk glazes over, about 1 minute longer.
